purpose: Curated irregular-structure source catalog for authority-grade benchmark discovery, native-roundtrip candidate scouting, and AI-learning oriented irregular-topology intake.

structure_families.why_it_matters: Visible transfer podiums expose sharp load-path jumps and are common in real high-rises.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: transfer-story demand ratio; column discontinuity penalty; load-path continuity

structure_families.why_it_matters: Canonical soft-story risk with clear drift concentration and strong regression value.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: story stiffness ratio; interstory drift concentration; P-Delta amplification

structure_families.why_it_matters: Offset core or mass eccentricity makes torsion and modal coupling easy to see and hard to fake.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: torsional irregularity index; corner drift spread; center-of-rigidity offset

structure_families.why_it_matters: Setbacks are a classic vertical irregularity that break stiffness and mass continuity.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: setback-level demand jump; story shear redistribution; mode-shape discontinuity

structure_families.why_it_matters: L/U/T-plan corners concentrate diaphragm shear and edge drift, making them useful for torsion-aware learning.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: corner drift amplification; diaphragm shear concentration; torsion/drift ratio

structure_families.why_it_matters: Megatall outriggers are a showpiece and stress coupled core-perimeter load sharing.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: core drift reduction; outrigger force share; top-out drift limit

structure_families.why_it_matters: Brace discontinuities create real weak-story behavior and expose load-path breaks beyond simple soft stories.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: brace force redistribution; weak-story check; drift concentration

structure_families.why_it_matters: Suspended floors and hanging columns are excellent demonstrations of reverse load flow and transfer demand.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: hanging-member force path completeness; transfer deflection; secondary-force amplification

structure_families.why_it_matters: Diagrid exoskeletons are visually strong and test perimeter stiffness reasoning under asymmetry.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: perimeter-to-core stiffness share; lateral efficiency; node stress concentration

structure_families.why_it_matters: Linked towers and split cores are a good coupled-system benchmark with clear visual wow.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: inter-tower drift differential; link force demand; coupling mode separation

structure_families.why_it_matters: Large overhangs make load reversal and support demand obvious.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: tip deflection; support moment ratio; gravity-lateral interaction

structure_families.why_it_matters: Abrupt mass changes shift modes and acceleration demands in a way learning systems should notice.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: mode-shape jump; story acceleration ratio; mass-stiffness mismatch index

structure_families.why_it_matters: A large internal void creates tricky diaphragm flow and strong perimeter demand.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: diaphragm shear flow around void; torsion amplification; perimeter concentration

structure_families.why_it_matters: Twist plus taper is a compact geometry stress test for shape-aware models.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: twist angle vs drift; stiffness-gradient sensitivity; coupled-mode ratio

structure_families.why_it_matters: Grade changes create practical irregularity that is easy to miss in high-level summaries.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: base reaction imbalance; story-to-story discontinuity; foundation demand skew

structure_families.why_it_matters: Nonorthogonal grids are a low-noise way to test geometry-aware load transfer.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: skew-induced secondary forces; connection demand; plan response coupling

structure_families.why_it_matters: Bridge skew broadens the track beyond towers and tests support asymmetry cleanly.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: support reaction spread; torsional twist under asymmetric load; span continuity

structure_families.why_it_matters: Curved bridges are a standard torsion problem and give the corpus a civil-structure edge case.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: curvature-induced torsion; lane-load asymmetry; lateral-torsional coupling

structure_families.why_it_matters: Irregular roof trusses are useful for big-span demos and partial-load response learning.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: deflection under partial loading; support reaction imbalance; member utilization spread

structure_families.why_it_matters: A shell-plus-core hybrid is the most visually distinctive capstone and the hardest geometry bucket.
structure_families.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: geometry-to-response consistency; shell membrane action; local instability sensitivity

source_records.title: MIDAS multifamily building original native MEB
source_records.why_it_matters: Visible transfer podiums expose sharp load-path jumps and are common in real high-rises.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: transfer-story demand ratio; column discontinuity penalty; load-path continuity
source_records.notes: Original MIDAS native binary model harvested from the public multifamily building support archive. Keep this for native roundtrip coverage, but do not treat it as canonical for the authority-grade irregular benchmark track until an original benchmark-native IFC/TCL/MGT/INP source is harvested.

metadata.benchmark_canonical_blocker: Original MIDAS native binary is useful for roundtrip/native-source coverage, but this track still requires a benchmark-native IFC/TCL/MGT/INP source for canonical authority-grade promotion.

source_records.title: Transfer podium tower local bridged graph from MIDAS multifamily building archive
source_records.why_it_matters: Visible transfer podiums expose sharp load-path jumps and are common in real high-rises.

source_records.notes: Execution-ready bridged local graph derived from a decoded MIDAS support archive preview. Stronger than a proxy, but still not a canonical PEER replacement.

metadata.canonical_promotion_blocker: Canonical benchmark model has not been collected yet; current local evidence is a bridged decoded-preview graph with native MEB support, while official PEER documentation is collected separately as reference-only evidence.

source_records.title: DesignSafe OpenSees pilotis RC frame package

source_records.notes: Execution-ready bridged local graph derived from a decoded MIDAS support archive preview. Stronger than a proxy, but still not the measured NHERI source.

source_records.title: OpenSees 606 m mega-tall building Model 1 TCL
source_records.why_it_matters: Offset core or mass eccentricity makes torsion and modal coupling easy to see and hard to fake.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: torsional irregularity index; corner drift spread; center-of-rigidity offset
source_records.notes: Harvested public OpenSees TCL package for the 606 m mega-tall building benchmark. This is benchmark-native model text and qualifies as canonical evidence for the torsionally eccentric core tower family.

metadata.upstream_provider: Tsinghua University benchmark page referenced by opstool docs
metadata.model_mesh_note: Model 1 coarse mesh; package also includes Model 2 refined mesh.

source_records.title: Offset-core megatall torsion bridged graph
source_records.why_it_matters: Offset core or mass eccentricity makes torsion and modal coupling easy to see and hard to fake.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: torsional irregularity index; corner drift spread; center-of-rigidity offset
source_records.notes: Execution-ready bridged graph for offset-core torsion and modal-coupling rehearsal. This remains useful for bridge-evidence runs, but canonical promotion is now backed by a harvested public TCL source.

metadata.canonical_promotion_blocker: Resolved by harvested public OpenSees TCL source from the published 606 m mega-tall building benchmark package.

source_records.title: TPU interference highrise response proxy

source_records.notes: Wind-response proxy for asymmetric tall-building response patterns; useful for learning, not a direct structural benchmark replacement.

source_records.title: Hanging-column tower benchmark candidate

source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: hanging-member force path completeness; transfer deflection; secondary-force amplification

source_records.title: Diagrid exoskeleton asymmetric tower candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: Diagrid exoskeletons are visually strong and test perimeter stiffness reasoning under asymmetry.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: perimeter-to-core stiffness share; lateral efficiency; node stress concentration
source_records.notes: Good for perimeter-stiffness reasoning and non-orthogonal member graph learning.

source_records.title: Split-core linked towers candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: Linked towers and split cores are a good coupled-system benchmark with clear visual wow.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: inter-tower drift differential; link force demand; coupling mode separation
source_records.notes: Linked towers and skybridges are strong coupled-system learning cases even when benchmark maturity is limited.

source_records.title: Cantilevered upper-volume tower candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: Large overhangs make load reversal and support demand obvious.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: tip deflection; support moment ratio; gravity-lateral interaction
source_records.notes: Good overhang and load-reversal family; needs benchmark-quality response data later.

source_records.title: Vertical mass jump tower candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: Abrupt mass changes shift modes and acceleration demands in a way learning systems should notice.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: mode-shape jump; story acceleration ratio; mass-stiffness mismatch index
source_records.notes: Measured building-response track for mass discontinuity and mode-shape shift.

source_records.title: Atrium void ring tower candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: A large internal void creates tricky diaphragm flow and strong perimeter demand.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: diaphragm shear flow around void; torsion amplification; perimeter concentration
source_records.notes: Large diaphragm voids are excellent for graph learning and diaphragm force-flow tracing.

source_records.title: Twisted tapered tower candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: Twist plus taper is a compact geometry stress test for shape-aware models.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: twist angle vs drift; stiffness-gradient sensitivity; coupled-mode ratio
source_records.notes: Strong geometry-irregularity family for shape-aware AI models and meshing workflows.

source_records.title: Sloped-site stepped podium tower candidate

source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: base reaction imbalance; story-to-story discontinuity; foundation demand skew

source_records.title: Skewed-column frame candidate

source_records.title: GTC public bridge bearing C04 raw MIDAS model

source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: support reaction spread; torsional twist under asymmetric load; span continuity
source_records.notes: Current best native MIDAS skew-support bridge source for real roundtrip and authority-facing bridge write-back validation.

source_records.title: GTC public curved bridge section A3 raw MIDAS model

source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: curvature-induced torsion; lane-load asymmetry; lateral-torsional coupling
source_records.notes: Public raw MIDAS bridge section with curved-plan potential and direct native write-back value.

source_records.title: GTC public curved bridge section E1-03 raw MIDAS model
source_records.why_it_matters: Curved bridges are a standard torsion problem and give the corpus a civil-structure edge case.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: curvature-induced torsion; lane-load asymmetry; lateral-torsional coupling
source_records.notes: Second public raw MIDAS bridge section for curved-plan bridge torsion breadth.

source_records.title: MIDAS support FCM bridge archive geometry proxy
source_records.why_it_matters: Curved bridges are a standard torsion problem and give the corpus a civil-structure edge case.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: curvature-induced torsion; lane-load asymmetry; lateral-torsional coupling
source_records.notes: Great AI-learning geometry proxy for segmental/FCM bridge authoring despite not being raw native MIDAS text.

source_records.title: IASS long-span irregular roof truss candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: Irregular roof trusses are useful for big-span demos and partial-load response learning.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: deflection under partial loading; support reaction imbalance; member utilization spread
source_records.notes: Good route for long-span roof irregularity and snapping/bifurcation-aware shell-truss learning.

source_records.title: IASS free-form shell core hybrid candidate
source_records.why_it_matters: A shell-plus-core hybrid is the most visually distinctive capstone and the hardest geometry bucket.
source_records.recommended_kpi_or_validation_angle: geometry-to-response consistency; shell membrane action; local instability sensitivity
source_records.notes: Best shell-heavy family for AI shape generalization beyond orthogonal frame systems.
